柏林（路透社）-德国潜水员在波罗的海寻找废弃的渔网时，偶然发现了纳粹军方在第二次世界大战期间使用的一台稀有的Enigma密码机，他们认为这是从一艘被破坏的潜艇上抛下的。
  水下考古学家弗洛里安·胡伯（Florian Huber）认为他们已经发现了纠缠在盖尔丁湾海底网中的打字机，因此很快意识到了这一发现的历史意义。
 “在过去的20年中，我取得了许多令人兴奋和奇怪的发现。但是我从来没有梦想过，有一天我们会找到其中一台传奇的Enigma机器。” Huber说。
 纳粹军人在第二次世界大战期间使用这些机器发送和接收秘密消息，但英国密码学家破解了密码，帮助盟国在控制大西洋的海上斗争中获得优势。
  在布莱奇利公园（Bletchley Park）密码破解中心，由艾伦·图灵（Alan Turing）领导的一支英国团队因破解密码，缩短战争时间并挽救了数千人的生命而倍受赞誉。
 在1945年5月德国投降前不久，大约50艘潜水艇或U型潜艇的船员奉命在丹麦边境附近的盖尔丁湾拆船，以免将其交给同盟国。销毁加密设备是订单的一部分。
 总部位于基尔的Submaris公司负责水下研究任务的Huber说：“我们怀疑我们的Enigma在这次活动中落得太大了。” 
总体而言，战争结束时，德国人在北海和波罗的海沉没了200多艘潜艇。  看起来像打字机的Enigma设备由一个键盘和一个扰乱消息的轮子组成。 尽管生产了数十万台机器，但已知只有几百台。 他们在拍卖会上以数万欧元的价格出售。  该发现是由代表世界自然基金会工作的潜水员完成的，目的是寻找危及海洋生物的废弃渔网，该发现将交给石勒苏益格的考古博物馆。
note_en:
BERLIN (Reuters) - German divers searching the Baltic Sea for discarded fishing nets have stumbled upon a rare Enigma cipher machine used by the Nazi military during World War Two which they believe was thrown overboard from a scuttled submarine.
  Thinking they had discovered a typewriter entangled in a net on the seabed of Gelting Bay, underwater archaeologist Florian Huber quickly realised the historical significance of the find.
 “I’ve made many exciting and strange discoveries in the past 20 years. But I never dreamt that we would one day find one of the legendary Enigma machines,” said Huber.
 The Nazi military used the machines to send and receive secret messages during World War Two but British cryptographers cracked the code, helping the Allies gain an advantage in the naval struggle to control the Atlantic.
  At Bletchley Park codebreaking centre, a British team led by Alan Turing is credited with unravelling the code, shortening the war and saving many thousands of lives.
 Shortly before Germany’s surrender in May 1945, the crews of about 50 submarines, or U-Boots, followed an order to scuttle their ships in Gelting Bay, near the Danish border, to avoid handing them to the Allies. Destroying encryption devices was part of the order.
 “We suspect our Enigma went overboard in the course of this event,” said Huber, of the Kiel-based company Submaris which leads underwater research missions.
  Overall, Germans sank more than 200 of their submarines in the North and Baltic Seas at the end of the war.
 The Enigma device, which looks like a typewriter, consisted of a keyboard and wheels which scrambled messages. Although several hundred thousand machines were produced, only a few hundred are known to exist. They sell at auction for tens of thousands of euros.
 The find, made by divers working on behalf of WWF aiming to find abandoned fishing nets that endanger marine life, will be given to the archaeology museum in Schleswig.
title: 潜水员在波罗的海发现纳粹WW2谜机
title_en: Divers Discover Nazi WW2 Enigma Machine in Baltic Sea

note:
雅加达-对于主要的美国科技公司来说，2020年表明印度尼西亚正准备成为下一个前沿领域。
 尽管冠状病毒大流行造成了破坏，但Facebook，Google和Microsoft之类的公司还是削减了对这家独角兽的投资，这些独角兽的估值超过10亿美元。今年，对印度尼西亚最大的科技公司Gojek以及电子商务平台Tokopedia和Bukalapak等公司的投资将达到数亿美元。
 就像有时被简称为GAFAM的美国科技公司以及亚马逊和苹果一样，它们也都在印度押宝，这些投资也承认印尼是一个不可忽视的市场。东南亚国家/地区是世界第四大人口大国，占该地区经济产出的三分之一。
  现在，由于北京与华盛顿的争执，作为亚洲最大市场的中国对美国科技公司而言似乎更具敌意，因此投资机会的重要性变得更大。
 Tokopedia和Bukalapak于11月成为美国科技资金的最新接受者。微软在本月早些时候宣布，已进行了战略投资。据报道，这家美国软件提供商与现有投资者GIC，新加坡主权财富基金以及PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi或当地媒体集团Emtek Group一起参与了Bukalapak的1亿美元融资。
  据提交给印尼当局的文件显示，Google于10月下旬或11月初与新加坡的淡马锡一起投资了Tokopedia。据说这两家投资者已向电子商务平台注资3.5亿美元。
 此前，Facebook和PayPal于6月份向Gojek注入了现金。 Google已经是hailer-cum-superapp的投资方，该公司提供从付款到食品交付和物流的服务。 
这些投资证明了印尼在其数字生态系统方面的发展以及增长潜力。根据Google，淡马锡（Temasek）和贝恩（Bain＆amp; Bain）的报告，其互联网经济在五年内增长了五倍多，到2020年达到440亿美元，是该地区迄今为止最大的。公司。报告称，到2025年，该市场的价值将达到1240亿美元，进一步巩固该群岛作为该地区最大的数字经济的地位。
 投资印度尼西亚的独角兽等成熟的初创公司是一次巨大的机会，只需一笔重大交易，就可以在生态系统中建立[强大]的地位[而不是仅仅逐步地有机地建立业务]。 BCG Digital Ventures的合伙人兼董事Hanno Stegmann说。
 它使美国公司能够在生态系统中建立明确的位置，当然，从此以后，就可以在关系基础上以更强大的足迹扩大业务范围，＆＃34; Stegmann添加了。
 一个典型的例子是Facebook与PayPal一起对Gojek的投资。当这家印度尼西亚公司宣布融资时，它表示关注的重点是＆＃34;支持付款和金融服务。＆＃34; Gojek的付款应用程序GoPay是该国最常用的数字钱包之一。
 但同时，Facebook也通过其WhatsApp子公司来推动自己的支付野心。
 虽然WhatsApp已经是群岛上一种流行的通信方式，但Gojek拥有更多的支付专业知识-并且它对一个横跨5,000公里，1,700个岛屿和1,300个种族的国家的当地细微差别有了更好的了解。
 ＆＃34;这笔投资将支持Facebook和Gojek的共同目标，即增强业务能力并推动整个群岛实现金融包容性。 Facebook宣布投资时，WhatsApp首席运营官Matt Idema说。与Gojek一起，我们相信可以带动数百万人进入印度尼西亚不断发展的数字经济。 
印度可能会预示即将发生的事情。 WhatsApp的支付服务于11月6日在世界第二人口大国推出，此前Facebook在4月斥资57亿美元对数字服务提供商Reliance Jio Platforms进行了投资。尽管到目前为止，这家美国公司对Gojek的投资收效甚微，但两家支付公司之间的合作似乎是不可避免的，就像WhatsApp Pay在Reliance的电子商务杂货店中将扮演重要角色一样零售商JioMart。
 与当地科技巨头的合作不断增加，可能会给美国科技公司带来该地区的收益。以Facebook为例，就每月活跃用户而言，亚太地区是其最大的用户群，今年第三季度占42％。但是，该地区占同期收入的19％。同样，在截至9月的9个月中，Google在亚太地区的收入仅占其总收入的18％。
 本地风险投资公司Alpha JWC的联合创始人兼普通合伙人杰弗里·乔（Jefrey Joe）认为新加坡海洋集团（Singapore）将印度尼西亚列在了地图上针对美国投资者。 Sea在纽约证券交易所上市，现在是东南亚最有价值的上市公司，对教育市场做出了很大的贡献，表明东南亚公司可以成为良好的公司，成长中的公司，并且具有强大的基本面。＆＃34;
 Sea的电子商务部门Shopee是印度尼西亚访问量最大的在线购物平台，而群岛是其数字钱包部门SeaMoney的最大市场。它的股价今年已涨了三倍多。
 美国科技公司表现出的兴趣也是印尼独角兽的喜人发展。
 对于一个人来说，资本是一种祝福。虽然如今初创公司周围的普遍气氛是一种节俭，但竞争依然激烈，这促使独角兽寻求更大的资金来源。 COVID-19增加了面临中断或希望在某些领域进行扩张以捕捉不断变化的消费者行为的公司的资金需求。
 美国的科技投资还允许印尼独角兽提高其在全球投资者中的知名度，如果他们决定在印度尼西亚境外上市，这将是非常需要的-也许是通过在国外市场双重上市。 
此外，对于已经存在约十年并发展到拥有规模的公司而言，替代性资本来源也受到限制。根据CB Insights的数据，Gojek的估值为100亿美元，Tokopedia的估值为70亿美元，Bukalapak的估值为35亿美元。
 ＆＃34;可以写这些支票的人不多，＆＃34; Alpha JWC的Joe表示，＆＃34;和传统的[投资者]可能对估值不满意。
 乔说，这为愿意大举投资的美国科技巨头带来了机会。 ＆＃34;最重要的是，[美国大公司]了解游戏。他们了解这些独角兽正在构建的内容，因为它们经历了[相同的过程]。
 大约五年前，GAFAM的部分业务开始涉足印尼。 2016年，苹果宣布投资4,400万美元在雅加达郊区建立一个应用开发人员培训中心。该学院于2018年完工，是该公司在东南亚的第一所学院。
 但这项投资更多是进入印尼智能手机市场的门票，而不是直接押注印尼数字经济。它使Apple能够遵守政府的要求，即在该国销售的手机必须具有最少数量的本地生产内容或组件。
 对印度尼西亚数字经济及其成长中的初创公司的第一个真正押注是在2018年初，当时Google投资了印度尼西亚最大的初创公司Gojek，这家位于山景城的公司目前持有该公司7％的股份。 39股。今年晚些时候，微软投资了Gojek位于新加坡的竞争对手Grab，后者最大的市场是印度尼西亚。
 如果两家东南亚公司决定合并-据说谈判已经进行了-美国公司将在跨越八个高增长国家的地区科技巨hem中拥有股份。 
美国科技公司不仅仅将赌注押在乘车服务和其他超级应用服务的增长上。相反，他们指望将印度尼西亚的云服务扩展作为关键战略驱动力之一。 BCG Digital Ventures的Stegmann说。云服务是美国科技公司的主要收入来源。
 有必要锁定某些大客户，并建立可靠的用例并在生态系统中建立信誉。这就是为什么这种投资策略非常聪明的原因。斯特格曼说。
 中国公司也对该细分市场垂涎三尺。据报道，阿里巴巴集团控股公司在印度尼西亚拥有两个数据中心，并计划明年建立第三个数据中心。
 Gojek和Tokopedia目前都使用Google Cloud，而Bukalapak在Microsoft投资后从Google切换到Microsoft的Azure。
  ＆＃34;归根结底，这是正常的＆＃34; Bukalapak首席执行官Rachmat Kaimuddin表示，将改用Microsoft的服务。 ＆＃34;如果您结婚了，基本上就是在家庭中寻找协同作用，＆＃34;他说。
 此前曾与Gojek商谈投资该公司的亚马逊，也计划在该群岛建立数据中心。
 波士顿咨询集团（Boston Consulting Group）在去年的一份报告中表示，印尼是亚太地区增长最快的[云]市场之一，预计未来五年的复合年增长率将从25亿美元增至25％。在2018年增加到2023年的8亿美元。由于冠状病毒大流行，电子商务和食品配送服务的普及使数据使用激增。 
该国已经是该地区最大的互联网经济体，是泰国第二大经济体的两倍多，但互联网普及率在东盟五国中最低。
 但是，随着政府去年完成的耗资15亿美元的Palapa环项目的竣工，该项目将印尼的所有岛屿连接成一个光纤网络，这种情况很快就会改变。
  独角兽也正在向农村地区扩展，创建更多数据。例如，布卡拉帕克（Bukalapak）正在帮助在遥远地方的妈妈和流行商店的数字化（以当地人的说法为准）。该公司首席执行官Kaimuddin说，他可以看到一个未来，其中通过warungs数字化生成的数据将与其电子商务平台生成的数据相同。
 Warung业务现在产生了商品总值的三分之一，＆＃34;他说。 ＆＃34;当然，随着[更多]交易产生[更多]数据。＆＃34;
 印尼还是美国与中国之间贸易争端的受益者，投资者现在已经厌倦了与北京相关的任何相关风险。因此，对亚洲的投资更有可能流向第二大市场印度和印度尼西亚。
 印度尼西亚拥有不结盟外交政策的好处-与印度相比，它与北京有自己的对立关系，因此，印度是一个相对中立的投资地。
 Preqin的数据显示，与2016年相比，2019年与美国投资者直接参与的印尼风险投资交易数量几乎是2016年的两倍，而同期的交易价值却翻了一番以上。 2020年的交易数量也可能超过2019年的数字。 
即使 美国 总统当选人 拜登 决定注销 更加 和解的 语气中带着 中国 ＆＃ 34; 投资者 想 肯定已经 改变了 亚洲 的投资 方式 ， ＆＃ 34; Alpha JWC的乔说。 ＆＃34;投资者在考虑多元化，考虑集中风险。  ＆＃34;这就是为什么一些较大的投资者认为...现在是[在印度尼西亚投资的合适时机]，因为规模已经证明了他们想要部署的资源以及他们对该市场的关注程度是合理的 。 对于印度尼西亚来说，这是一个好信号。＆＃34;
note_en:
JAKARTA -- For the major U.S. tech companies, 2020 showed that Indonesia is shaping up to be the next frontier.
 Despite the disruption caused by the coronavirus pandemic, the likes of Facebook, Google and Microsoft have cut a swath of deals to invest in the archipelago's unicorns -- private companies valued at over $1 billion. Investments in companies such as Gojek, Indonesia's largest tech company, as well as e-commerce platforms Tokopedia and Bukalapak this year are set to reach hundreds of millions of dollars.
 Just as the same U.S. tech companies, sometimes grouped under the acronym GAFAM along with Amazon and Apple, are also betting big on India, the investments acknowledge that Indonesia is a market too big to ignore. The Southeast Asian country is the fourth most populous in the world, accounting for a third of the region's economic output.
  The importance of the investment opportunities is even greater now that China, Asia's largest market, seems a more hostile territory for U.S. tech companies because of Beijing's spat with Washington.
 Tokopedia and Bukalapak became the latest recipients of U.S. tech money in November. Microsoft announced earlier in the month it had made a "strategic investment" in Bukalapak, with the American software provider taking part in a reported $100 million funding round alongside existing investors GIC, Singapore's sovereign wealth fund, and PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi, or Emtek Group, a local media conglomerate.
  Google invested in Tokopedia, alongside Singapore's Temasek, in late October or early November, according to filings made to Indonesian authorities. Both investors are said to have pumped $350 million into the e-commerce platform.
 The investments follow Facebook's and PayPal's cash injections into Gojek in June. Google is already an investor in the ride hailer-cum-superapp, which offers services from payments to food delivery and logistics.
 The investments are testament to how far Indonesia has come in terms of its digital ecosystem, and to its growth potential. Its internet economy has grown more than fivefold in five years to be worth $44 billion in 2020, by far the biggest in the region, according to a report by Google, Temasek and Bain & Company. The market will be worth $124 billion in 2025, according to the report, further cementing the archipelago as the region's top digital economy.
 Investing in established startups like Indonesia's unicorns is "a great opportunity, with one significant transaction, to build [a strong] position in the ecosystem [rather] than just gradually building the business organically," said Hanno Stegmann, partner and director at BCG Digital Ventures.
 It allows the U.S. firms to "establish a clear position in the ecosystem, and of course from that, expanding the business presence with a way stronger footprint on a relationship basis," Stegmann added.
 A case in point is Facebook's investment in Gojek, alongside PayPal. When the Indonesian company announced the funding, it said the focus was on "supporting payments and financial services." Gojek's payment app GoPay is one of the country's most used digital wallets.
 But it also comes with Facebook pushing its own payment ambitions through its WhatsApp subsidiary.
 While WhatsApp is already a popular method of communication in the archipelago, Gojek has more payments expertise -- and it also has a better understanding of local nuances in a country that spans 5,000 km, 1,700 islands and 1,300 ethnic groups.
 "This investment will support Facebook and Gojek's shared goal of empowering businesses and driving financial inclusion across the archipelago," said Matt Idema, WhatsApp COO, when Facebook announced the investment. "Together with Gojek, we believe we can bring millions of people into Indonesia's growing digital economy."
  India could offer a sign of things to come. WhatsApp's payment service launched in the world's second most populous country on Nov. 6, following Facebook's $5.7 billion investment in digital services provider Reliance Jio Platforms in April. While the U.S. company's investment in Gojek has so far yielded little, a collaboration between both payment firms seems inevitable down the road, much as WhatsApp Pay is expected to play an important role at Reliance's e-commerce grocery retailer JioMart.
 Increased collaborations with local tech giants may provide the U.S. tech companies a leg up in their earnings in the region. Facebook, for instance, counts Asia-Pacific as its largest user base in terms of monthly active users, accounting for 42% in the third quarter of this year. However, the region accounted for 19% of the revenue in the same period. Similarly, Google only made 18% of its revenue in the Asia-Pacific region in the nine months ended in September.
 Jefrey Joe, co-founder and general partner at local venture capital Alpha JWC, credits Singapore's Sea Group for putting "Indonesia on the map" for the U.S. investors. Sea, listed on the New York Stock Exchange and now the most valuable listed company in Southeast Asia, "has done a favor of educating the market, showing Southeast Asian companies can be good companies, growing, [and have] strong fundamentals."
 Sea's e-commerce arm Shopee is the most visited online shopping platform in Indonesia, and the archipelago is the biggest market for its digital wallet arm SeaMoney. Its share price has more than quadrupled this year.
 The interest shown by U.S. tech companies is also a welcome development for the Indonesian unicorns.
 For one, the capital is a blessing. While the general atmosphere surrounding startups nowadays is one of frugality, competition remains intense, driving the unicorns to seek bigger war chests. COVID-19 has increased the need for capital among companies that have faced disruptions or want to expand in some areas to capture changing consumer behavior.
 U.S. tech investment also allows Indonesian unicorns to raise their profile among global investors, something that will be much needed if and when they decide to go public outside Indonesia -- perhaps via a dual listing in a foreign market.
  In addition, alternative sources of capital are limited for companies that have been around for some 10 years and grown to the scale they have. Gojek is valued at $10 billion, Tokopedia at $7 billion and Bukalapak at $3.5 billion, according to CB Insights.
 "Not many people can write those checks," Joe of Alpha JWC said, "and conventional [investors] might not be comfortable with the valuations."
 That has opened an opportunity for the U.S. tech heavyweights willing to splash the cash to step in, Joe said. "Most importantly, [the big U.S. companies] understand the game. They understand what these unicorns are building because they have gone through [the same process]."
 Parts of GAFAM started to venture into Indonesia around five years ago. In 2016, Apple announced a $44 million investment to establish a training center for app developers on the outskirts of Jakarta. The academy, completed in 2018, was the company's first in Southeast Asia.
 But the investment was more of an entrance ticket into Indonesia's smartphone market rather than a direct bet on Indonesia's digital economy. It allowed Apple to comply with a government requirement that phones sold in the country had to have a minimum amount of locally produced content or components.
 The first real bet on Indonesia's digital economy and its growing startups came in early 2018, when Google invested in Gojek, Indonesia's biggest startup, with the Mountain View-based company now holding 7% of the company's shares. Later in the year, Microsoft invested in Grab, Gojek's Singapore-based rival whose largest market is Indonesia.
 Should both Southeast Asian companies decide to merge -- talks have reportedly advanced -- the U.S. firms will have a stake in a regional tech behemoth spanning eight high-growth countries.
  U.S. tech companies are not merely betting on the growth of ride hailing and other superapp services. Rather they are counting on the expansion of cloud services in Indonesia as "one of the key strategic drivers" for their investments, said Stegmann of BCG Digital Ventures. Cloud services are a major revenue driver for U.S. tech companies.
 There is a need to "lock in certain big customers, and to build credible use cases and to build the credibility in the ecosystem. That's why this investment strategy is very smart," said Stegmann.
 Chinese companies are also covetous of the segment. Alibaba Group Holding has two data centers in Indonesia and plans a third next year, according to reports.
 Both Gojek and Tokopedia currently use Google Cloud, while Bukalapak switched from Google to Microsoft's Azure after Microsoft's investment.
  "At the end of the day, it is normal" to switch to Microsoft's service, said Rachmat Kaimuddin, Bukalapak CEO. "If you get married, you basically try to find synergies within the family," he said.
 Amazon, which was previously in talks with Gojek to invest in the company, also has plans to build data centers in the archipelago.
 Boston Consulting Group said in a report last year that Indonesia was "one of the fastest-growing [cloud] markets in the APAC region, with a projected compound annual growth rate of 25% over the next five years, from $0.2 billion in 2018 to $0.8 billion in 2023." Data use is exploding from the popularization of e-commerce and food delivery services as a result of the coronavirus pandemic.
 The country is already the region's biggest internet economy, more than twice the size of No. 2 Thailand, but internet penetration is the lowest among the ASEAN-5 nations.
 That could soon change, however, with the completion last year of the government's $1.5 billion Palapa Ring project, which wires all of Indonesia's islands into a fiber-optic network.
  The unicorns are also branching out to rural areas, creating more data. Bukalapak, for example, is helping the digitization of mom and pop shops --  warungs in the local parlance -- in far-flung places. Company CEO Kaimuddin said he can see a future in which the data generated through the digitization of warungs is equivalent to that generated by its e-commerce platform.
 The warung business "now generates a third of gross merchandise value," he said. "Of course, with [more] transaction comes [more] data."
 Indonesia has also been a beneficiary of the trade spat between the U.S. and China, with investors now weary of the risks related to anything Beijing related. Investment into Asia, therefore, is more likely to head toward India and Indonesia, the next biggest markets.
 Indonesia has the bonus of a nonalignment foreign policy -- making it a relatively neutral ground for investment as compared to India, which has its own antagonistic relationship with Beijing.
 Data from Preqin shows that the number of venture capital deals in Indonesia with direct participation from U.S.-based investors nearly doubled in 2019 compared to 2016, while the deal value in the same period more than doubled. 2020 may also see the number of deals overtake 2019 figures.
 Even if U.S. president-elect Joe Biden decides to strike a more reconciliatory tone with China, "the way investors think about investments in Asia has definitely changed," Joe of Alpha JWC said. "Investors are thinking more about diversification, thinking about concentration risk.
 "That is why some of the larger investors think ... it is the right time [to invest in Indonesia] because the size already justifies the resources that they want to deploy, the attention that they want to put to this market. That is a good signal for Indonesia."
title: 随着Facebook，Google和Microsoft对印尼科技公司的大量投资，该国的互联网经济已增长至$ 44B，是2015年的五倍多
title_en: As Facebook, Google, and Microsoft invest heavily in Indonesian tech companies, the nation's internet economy has grown to $44B, over fivefold from 2015

note:
我们现在正处于疫苗灭亡的开始：监管部门的批准以及向人群的实际分发/推广。辉瑞/ BioNTech和Moderna疫苗的数据看起来仍然不错（这是Moderna疫苗后免疫应答寿命长的新报告），而J＆amp; J和Novavax的努力仍在报告中。 AZ /牛津大学的候选人更像是一个难题，这要归功于他们在临床工作上的沟通非常差（本身就遭受了一些基本问题）。
 现在我们必须让人们接受它们。调查继续显示出很多人（至少）处于“为什么不先考虑”这一类别。我倾向于认为，随着疫苗剂量的增加，越来越多的人将排队等候注射疫苗，但这仍有待观察。不过，我想强调一些我们都需要记住的东西。
 UCSF的Bob Wachter前几天在Twitter上有一个关于疫苗推出的很好的话题，他指出的一个好点就是这一点。我们正在谈论治疗非常大的人群，这意味着您将看到在大样本中看到的通常的死亡率和发病率。具体来说，如果您带走1000万人，并且只是在上臂上来回摇动，那么在接下来的两个月中，您将看到大约4,000次心脏病发作。约4,000笔。超过9,000种新的癌症诊断。在这1000万例死亡中，约有14,000人会死于正常的全因死亡率。没有人会注意到。现在，无论如何，很多人都死了并且生病了。
 但是，如果您带走了这1000万人，而是给他们注射了新疫苗，则确实存在危险，即心脏病，癌症诊断和死亡将归因于疫苗。我的意思是，如果您的人口足够多，那么您实际上会遇到有人接种疫苗并在第二天死亡的情况（就像他们*没有*得到疫苗一样）。不过，要说服该人的朋友和亲戚缺乏联系可能很难。事后审验ergo proper hoc是人类逻辑最有力的谬论之一，我们不会很快消除它。特别是在疫苗方面。我认为，我们能做的最好的事情就是设法提前知道。让人们知道这样的事情将会发生，因为人们在这个世界上不断生病和死亡。关键是一旦接种疫苗，他们是生病还是以明显更高的速度死亡。
 对于首次推出的疫苗，没有出现这样的安全信号（Moderna和Pfizer / BioNTech）。实际上，随着越来越多的人接种疫苗，我们应该看到对死亡率和发病率的作用正好相反。迄今为止，冠状病毒大流行中的超额死亡数字令人震惊（在美国超过30万），我当然认为大规模疫苗接种是我们必须将其恢复正常的最有效方法。
 但是，如果我们听到人们因接种疫苗后因心脏病发作而倒下的消息大叫，那将很难做到。振作起来。
  特别是对于COVID疫苗，公共卫生官员向公众媒体报道副作用是否没有抑制作用？公共卫生的目标是使所有人都接种疫苗，即使费用是数百或数千的严重副作用。报告任何严重的副作用都可能导致您假设放大。我同意扩增会危害公共健康，但是如果不良事件报告要么被善意的当地医生自行压制，又被当局压制了，那么如何收集各种疫苗相对安全性的准确数据呢？ 
最早接种疫苗的人群之一将是非常老的人群和合并症患者。无论如何，他们中的许多人可能会死于其他原因，但是他们的死亡肯定有可能归因于疫苗。如果不控制年龄，那么接种疫苗的人的死亡率可能会超过未受保护人群的死亡率。
  EUA是否会影响4期研究的报名？当您谈论为3.4亿人接种疫苗时，显然会有大量的时间用于流行病学研究，但是在最高的发病率风险之后，第四阶段的研究将为我们提供最快的发病率读数。第4阶段读数的时间表比耸人听闻的新闻报道的时间表要长得多，但我认为没有办法解决。科学家的工作是冷静地指向数据，而不在没有数据的情况下发表声明（留给政客）。
  正是这样的想法使我陷入了困境。虽然您建议开始共享此信息以“接种”针对事后谬论的建议确实有道理，但我们所有人都可以期待反vaxxer人群的“反消息”关于这些疫苗将如何杀死我们所有人的某种“大药房掩盖”……我唯一的希望（除了黑暗的想法是，“这些人”将自己的野猪吊起……）是足够的人足够理性（并且个人利益）服用疫苗，我们获得了足够的畜群免疫力，从而降低了R0…
  我无法控制其他人选择做什么来保护自己。我可以控制自己的所作所为，并且可以影响那些与我亲密的人和我的家人。我们将尽快接种所有疫苗。我讨厌这样说，但是如果其他人选择愚蠢并相信妖精的尾巴反对所有更好的证据，那就是他们的葬礼，而不是我的葬礼。
  疗养院中的许多人很可能处于死亡边缘，并且其中有10％的人因接种疫苗而发烧，头痛和不适感可能比其他情况要推迟几个小时或一天。这将是最难辩驳的情况。
 另外，有些已经接种疫苗的人已经处于月食期，在那里他们感染了SARS-CoV-2，但尚未脱落病毒，或者在接种后一周左右暴露出来，如果他们没有任何抵抗力。其中一些人声称他们在接种疫苗后感染了病毒。特设后遗症的终极案例。
  在某种程度上，我不在乎阴谋论者怎么说。这些mRNA疫苗的好处（至少从我们现在的数据来看）是非常有效的。因此，我不需要依靠其他人接种疫苗来通过免疫力保护自己。我只需要自己接种疫苗即可保护自己。当然，我希望生活恢复正常，但除此之外，我还不想生病和死亡。不管其他人的疫苗理论如何，后者的目标似乎都是可以实现的。 
史蒂夫·班农（Steve Bannon）：“真正的反对者是媒体。处理这些问题的方法是在该地区充斥粪便。”
  这就是我们所看到的。他的特工和有用的白痴无处不在，包括此博客的评论部分。它们很容易被发现，半脑子的人都可以通过肚进行分类，但是不幸的是，错误信息的确对许多人有效。
 这是“新异常”，我们一直坚持下去。令人遗憾的是，正如Bannon所建议的那样，MSM在很大程度上是垃圾，但是“用粪便淹没区域”是无法解决的方法。
  我从辉瑞和Moderna试验的许多人那里都听说过，尽管SE较为轻微。有些人几天后才出现类似流感的真实症状。这对于有合并症的老年人可能是个问题。
  如果我不在高危人群中，为什么要服用？还没有人知道长期影响。严重的问题。
  你不应该接受它。您可能会拒绝给高危人士接种疫苗的机会，因为剂量会受到限制。
 在接下来的几个月中，将有数以百万计的人得到疫苗接种，其中包括希望属于高危人群的人。届时将有更多数据供您做出决定。 
因此，根据您的推理，如果您对足够多的人口进行抽样，那么许多特定类型的人将死于多种原因，我们需要向人们解释这一点。对于我们现在已经被认为死于covid而没有其他任何原因的大样本人来说，情况会不是一样吗？我认为这很有趣，因为在一种情况下，它被用来支持一个职位，但在另一些人中，它被称为疯狂的阴谋理论家。您如何调和？
  更加关注的问题是疫苗是否导致神经系统疾病和疾病增加。这些疫苗的功效和安全性似乎很高。这些数字在更多人群中的侵蚀（应该发生）会对谁选择接种疫苗产生重大影响吗？
 还有一些剩余的问题需要尽快解决。那些已经患有新型冠状病毒的人应该接种疫苗吗？如果不是的话，所需疫苗的剂量数目将大大下降。据估计，感染这种病毒的人数是被检测到的人数的8倍（到秋季初，所以在过去两个月中这些病毒的数量已经大大减少了）。可能已有约1亿人患有新型冠状病毒。
 另一个未解决的问题是，接种疫苗是否会阻止人传播疫苗。似乎大多数公共卫生专家都明智地建议即使在疫苗广泛普及后也要戴口罩（至少要等到对这个问题有确切的答案之前）。
  到底是什么虚假的副作用！？您可能会产生副作用，也可能没有副作用，在药物试验中就不会出现“虚假效果”之类的事情。这是一个带有认知失调的偏见研究人员的例子，当您不希望出现副作用时，您将其重命名为“假”，而由于不想包含它们而盲目地忽略它们。我仍在等待分离出“新致命病毒” Sars CoV2（不是Sars Cov 2002或健康人肚脐中发现的冠状病毒）的证据和科学证据，以及证明您是否接受的研究活的可传播病毒并将其气雾剂传播给健康的人，使他们获得“ covid”（“新的致命疾病”）。
  我在社交圈中遇到了一些困难，这些人坚称他们不是抗病毒药，但他们敦促人们在无法建立长期安全性的情况下谨慎服用新疫苗。这很难解决，因为从表面上看它看起来很合理。我认为让我们担心从未有过的未知未来影响的可能性是不合理的，我们从未见过这种保护胜过巨大的已知的covid风险（包括直接和可能的长期健康），但是很难一一列举即使所有间接证据都表明该风险很小，也存在未知风险。无论如何，尽管争论似乎无济于事，但我希望一旦疫苗被推广到最初的数百万人中，他们会有所缓解。最近几年可能只会加剧人们的悲观情绪。
  请别笑。我在英国，是一位退休的麻醉师（医师，所以您可以说是麻醉师/强化医生）。我是疫苗的狂热爱好者。我正在接受血小板增多症的化学治疗，所以我认为该疫苗的排名很高。所以突然我很紧张。任何智慧的话，将不胜感激！ 
下次我评论时，请在此浏览器中保存我的姓名，电子邮件和网站。
note_en:
We’re in the beginning of the vaccine endgame now: regulatory approval and actual distribution/rollout into the population. The data for the Pfizer/BioNTech and Moderna vaccines continue to look good (here’s  a new report on the longevity of immune response after the Moderna one), with the J&J and Novavax efforts still to report. The AZ/Oxford candidate is more of a puzzle, thanks to some very poor communication about their clinical work (which suffered from some fundamental problems itself).
 Now we have to get people to take them. Surveys continue to show a good number of people who are (at the very least) in the “why don’t you take it first” category. I tend to think that as vaccine dosing becomes reality that more people will get in line for a shot, but that remains to be seen. I wanted to highlight something that we’ll all need to keep in mind, though.
 Bob Wachter of UCSF had a very good thread on Twitter about vaccine rollouts the other day, and one of the good points he made was  this one. We’re talking about treating very, very large populations, which means that you’re going to see the usual run of mortality and morbidity that you see across large samples. Specifically, if you take 10 million people and  just wave your hand back and forth over their upper arms, in the next two months you would expect to see about 4,000 heart attacks. About 4,000 strokes. Over 9,000 new diagnoses of cancer. And about 14,000 of that ten million will die, out of usual all-causes mortality. No one would notice. That’s now many people die and get sick anyway.
 But if you took those ten million people and gave them a new vaccine instead, there’s a real danger that those heart attacks, cancer diagnoses, and deaths will be attributed to the vaccine. I mean, if you reach a large enough population, you are literally going to have cases where someone gets the vaccine and drops dead the next day (just as they would have if they *didn’t* get the vaccine). It could prove difficult to convince that person’s friends and relatives of that lack of connection, though.  Post hoc ergo propter hoc is one of the most powerful fallacies of human logic, and we’re not going to get rid of it any time soon. Especially when it comes to vaccines. The best we can do, I think, is to try to get the word out in advance. Let people know that such things are going to happen, because people get sick and die constantly in this world. The key will be whether they are getting sick or dying at a noticeably higher rate once they have been vaccinated.
 No such safety signals have appeared for the first vaccines to roll out (Moderna and Pfizer/BioNTech). In fact, we should be seeing the exact opposite effects on mortality and morbidity as more and more people get vaccinated. The excess-death figures so far in the coronavirus pandemic have been appalling (well over 300,000 in the US), and I certainly think mass vaccination is the most powerful method we have to knock that back down to normal.
 That’s going to be harder to do, though, if we get screaming headlines about people falling over due to heart attacks after getting their vaccine shots. Be braced.
  For the COVID vaccine specifically, is there not a disincentive for public health officials to report side effects to the public media? The public health goal is for everyone to get vaccinated, even if the cost is hundreds or a few thousand serious side effects. Reporting any serious side effects could lead to the amplification you postulate. While I agree that amplification would be bad for public health, how can accurate data be collected on the comparative safety of the various vaccines if adverse event reporting is either self-suppressed by well-meaning local physicians, or suppressed in aggregate by authorities?
  One of the first cohorts to have the vaccine will be the very aged and those with co-morbidities. Many of them would be expected to die from other causes anyway, but there is certainly a likelihood that their deaths will be ascribed to the vaccine. There is a possibility that a death rate of vaccinated people will seem to exceed that of the unprotected population if the figures aren’t controlled for age.
  Does EUA affect the enrollment of Phase 4 studies? When you’re talking about vaccinating 340M people there will obviously be plenty of time for epidemiological studies but Phase 4 studies following the highest morbidity risk people would give us the quickest readout on the morbidity rates. The timeline of a Phase 4 readout is much longer than the timeline of a sensationalist news piece but I don’t see a way around it. It’s the job of scientists to calmly point to the data and not make statements in the absence of data (leave that to politicians).
  It is thoughts like this that bring out the misanthrope in me… While your suggestion to start sharing this info to “inoculate” against the post-hoc fallacy does make sense, we can all anticipate the “counter messaging” from the anti-vaxxer crowd about some sort of “big pharma coverup” about how these vaccines are going to kill us all… My only hope (beyond the dark ones about “these people” being hoist on their own petard…) is that enough people are sufficiently rational (and self-interested) to take the vaccine and we achieve sufficient herd immunity to lower the R0…
  I cannot control what other people choose to do to protect themselves. I can control what I do and I can influence those close to me and in my family. We will all get vaccinated as soon as it is possible to do so. I hate to say it, but if someone else chooses to be foolish and believe in fairy tails against all better evidence, then it’s their funeral and not mine.
  It is very likely that many people in nursing homes are on the edge of death and that the 10% of so that have fever, headache, and malaise from vaccination might be pushed over a few hours or a day than otherwise. This will be the hardest situation to argue against.
 Also some people who are vaccinated will already be in an eclipse period where they are infected with SARS-CoV-2 but are not yet shedding virus or who will be exposed in the week or so after vaccination when they have not built up any resistance. Some these will claim they got the virus after the vaccination. The ultimate case of post hoc ergo propter hoc.
  On some level, I don’t care what the conspiracy theorists have to say. The nice thing about these mRNA vaccines (at least from the data we have right now) is that they’re REALLY efficacious. So I don’t need to count on others getting the vaccine in order to protect myself via heard immunity. I just need to get the vaccine myself, at which point I’m protected. Of course I want life to go back to normal, but more than that I want to not get sick and die. And that latter goal seems to be achievable, regardless of others’ vaccine theories.
  Steve Bannon : “The real opposition is the media. And the way to deal with them is to flood the zone with shit.”
  That’s what we’ve been seeing. His operatives and useful idiots are literally everywhere , including this blog’s comment section. They’re easy enough to spot , and anyone with half a brain can sort thru the tripe , but the misinfo is indeed effective on large numbers , unfortunately.
 It’s the “new abnormal” , and we’re stuck with it. The sad thing is that the MSM is , to a large extent , garbage , as Bannon suggests , but “flooding the zone with shit” is no way to fix it.
  I have heard from a number of people on both the Pfizer and Moderna trials that although SEs are mild. Some people have real flu-like symptoms for a couple of days. This could be an issue in the elderly with co-morbidity.
  If I am not in the high risk group why would I take it? Nobody knows long term effects yet. Serious question.
  You shouldn’t take it. You would just be denying a high-risk person the opportunity to be vaccinated , since doses will be limited for some time.
 Within the next several months , millions of people will have been vaccinated , including, hopefully, all in the high-risk groups. There will be a lot more data to base your decision on then.
  So base on your reasoning of so many people of certain types are going to die from a myriad of causes if you sample a big enough population and we need to explain that to people. Wouldn’t the same be true for the big sample that we have now who supposedly died from covid and not any of the other reasons? I see this as interesting because in one case it is used to support a position but in the other folks are labeled as crazy conspiracy theorists. How do you reconcile that?
  The more focused concern is whether there is an increase in neurological conditions and diseases as a result of the vaccines. The efficacy and safety of these vaccines appears to be sky high. Would an erosion in these figures in a larger population (should they occur) have any significant affect on who choses to get the vaccine?
 There are some remaining questions that need to be answered in short order. Should those who already have had the novel coronavirus get the vaccine? If not the number of doses of the vaccine needed drops substantially. One estimate is that 8 times as many people have had the virus as has been detected (through early Fall so those numbers may have gone done substantially over the last two months). It is possible that around 100 million people have already had the novel coronavirus.
 Another unanswered question is whether getting the vaccine prevents a person from spreading it. It seems like most public health experts wisely advise to keep wearing masks even when a vaccine becomes widely available (at least until a definite answer is avaliable for this question).
  What the hell is a false side effect!? You either have side effects or you don’t, there are no such things as “false effects” when it comes to drug trials . That is a example of biased researcher$ with cognitive dissonance, when you don’t want side effects so you rename them ( false) and blindly ignore them as you don’t want to include them. I’m still waiting for the evidence and scientific proof of the isolation of a ” new deadly virus” Sars CoV2 ( not Sars Cov 2002 or the corona virus found up the navel cavity of healthy people) and the studies that prove if you take said live transmissible virus and aerosol it to healthy people they get ” covid” (a “new deadly disease”).
  I have been having some difficulty in my social circles with people who insist they’re not anti-vaxxers but are urging people to be cautious about taking a new vaccine when long-term safety isn’t established. This has been hard to deal with since on its surface it looks rational. I think it’s pretty irrational to let worry about the chance of an unknown future effect we’ve never seen outweigh protection from the gigantic, known risks of covid (both to immediate and possibly long-term health), but it’s hard to put a number on an unknown risk even if all circumstantial evidence suggests the risk is very small. Anyway, while arguing doesn’t seem to get anywhere with that I hope that once vaccines have been rolled out to the first few million people that they will calm down a bit. The last few years might just be amping up people’s pessimism.
  Please don’t laugh . I’m in the UK , I’m a retired anaeasthetist (a physician , so what you would call an anaesthesiologist / intensivist) . I’m a huge vaccine enthusiast . I’m on chemo for thrombocythaemia , so I think I’m quite high on the list for the vaccine. So suddenly I’m very nervous . Any words of wisdom would be appreciated !
   Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.
title: 随着疫苗的推出，请准备好应对不良的副作用
title_en: As Vaccines Are Rolled Out, Get Ready for False Side Effects
